description: Fix resource leaks and correctness bugs in the browser extension: unbounded WebSocket frame map in network capture, promise chain leak in tab-state plugin locks, stale window IDs in side panel toggle, and unbounded nonce Set in log relay injection.

userStories.title: Fix wsFramesByRequestId leak on WebSocket close
userStories.description: As a developer using network capture, I want closed WebSocket connections to be cleaned up so that long capture sessions do not leak memory.
acceptanceCriteria: A Network.webSocketClosed CDP event handler is added that deletes the corresponding entry from wsFramesByRequestId
The webSocketClosed event name is added to the list of CDP event subscriptions in startCapture
Existing unit tests for network-capture still pass
userStories.notes:
File: platform/browser-extension/src/network-capture.ts

The wsFramesByRequestId Map (line ~113) stores WebSocket frame buffers keyed by requestId. Entries are added on Network.webSocketCreated (line ~321) and frames appended on Network.webSocketFrameReceived (line ~334), but there is no handler for Network.webSocketClosed. Over long capture sessions with many WS connections opening and closing, this map grows unboundedly.

Fix: In the chrome.debugger.onEvent handler (the big switch/if chain starting around line 216), add a case for 'Network.webSocketClosed' that calls wsFramesByRequestId.delete(params.requestId). Also ensure the event handler filters for this event name alongside the existing WS events.

The stopCapture function (around line 165) already clears the entire map, and getWsFrames with clear=true also clears it, but those only run on explicit stop/clear. The webSocketClosed handler provides incremental cleanup during active capture.

userStories.title: Fix promise chain leak in pluginLocks
userStories.description: As a developer, I want plugin tab-state serialization locks to not accumulate unbounded promise chains so that long-running sessions do not leak memory.
acceptanceCriteria: The withPluginLock function resets the lock to a resolved promise after the operation completes, breaking the chain
Concurrent calls to withPluginLock for the same plugin still serialize correctly (no parallel execution)

userStories.title: Fix stale window IDs in side panel toggle
userStories.description: As a user, I want the side panel toggle to work correctly even if a window was closed while the service worker was suspended.
acceptanceCriteria: The toggleSidePanel function validates that the window still exists before checking openWindows membership
If chrome.windows.get throws (window gone), the stale ID is removed from openWindows and the panel is opened
userStories.notes:
File: platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel-toggle.ts

The openWindows Set (line 6) tracks which windows have the side panel open. It is populated by chrome.sidePanel.onOpened (line 23) and cleaned by chrome.sidePanel.onClosed (line 27) and chrome.windows.onRemoved (line 31). However, in MV3, the service worker can be suspended — if a window is closed during suspension, onRemoved is missed and the stale ID persists in the Set.

The toggleSidePanel function (line 8) checks `openWindows.has(windowId)` — if a stale ID matches, it calls close() instead of open(), causing the toggle to appear broken.

Fix: In toggleSidePanel, before the openWindows.has check, verify the window actually exists. If chrome.windows.get(windowId) throws, delete the stale ID from openWindows and fall through to the open() path.

Alternatively, since the Set is in-memory and the service worker can restart at any time (losing all state), consider always checking the actual side panel state via the Chrome API rather than relying on the in-memory Set. But the simplest fix is the get() validation.

userStories.title: Fix unbounded nonce Set in injectLogRelay
userStories.description: As a developer, I want log relay injection to not accumulate stale nonces in the ISOLATED world so that re-injections during plugin updates do not leak memory.
acceptanceCriteria: The injectLogRelay function clears old nonces from the ISOLATED world nonces Set before adding the new one, OR checks if the relay is already installed and skips redundant injection
Console log relay from MAIN world to background still works correctly after a plugin update triggers re-injection
userStories.notes:
File: platform/browser-extension/src/iife-injection.ts, lines 107-167

The injectLogRelay function injects an ISOLATED-world content script that listens for opentabs:console CustomEvents and relays them to the background. Each injection generates a crypto.randomUUID() nonce and adds it to a `nonces` Set on the document object in the ISOLATED world (line ~126). The listener checks nonces.has(nonce) to verify the event came from a legitimate injection.

The problem: each re-injection (triggered by plugin updates or adapter hash changes) adds a NEW nonce without clearing old ones. The nonces Set grows by one entry per re-injection per tab. Old nonces are never removed.

Fix options (pick the simplest):
1. Clear the nonces Set before adding the new nonce. Since re-injection replaces the previous console interceptors in MAIN world (which use the new nonce), old nonces are no longer valid anyway.
2. Check if the relay listener is already installed (e.g., via a flag on the document) and skip re-injection entirely. The relay just forwards events — it doesn't need to be reinstalled on adapter updates.

Option 2 is cleaner if the relay truly doesn't need updating. Option 1 is simpler if there's any reason the relay code might change between versions.
